have read with deep interest
the logical criticism of Mr.. Sunday
by Jessie Shirley, published in
Tuesdays Press, and she deserved
the highest commentator for the
effor,.


1t may be true, as Adolph Ca
meron says. that Miss Shirley
keeps her show open on Sunday.
So do the street cars run on Sun
day, and all the great railway
trains, and all the mines and smell
ters and thousand other kinds of
enterprises run on Sunday. The
people want 1t that way. The Sun
day of a hundred years ago has
been changed to Sunday of
recreation to those who do not
have to work, because the people
want it that way. Even the base
ball teams play on Sunday, and not
even the evangelical Billy has been
able to reform his associates on the
diamond. The people want II and
all the preachers in Christiandom
cannot prevent it. One might as
well expect good general to de-
lay battle with an enemy because
the opportunity happened to come
on Sunday. ..... ......


There may be some immoral in
fluences connected with theaters,
but the reverse Is the rule. And
there are many plays. like the
"Sign of the Cross" which con-
tains more moral sentiment than
all the sermons Billy Sunday ever
preached


To listen to the rantings of Billy
Sunday one might be led to be-
lieve that local option and tenn
perence are synonymous terms.
But they are not Excessive drink
ing can only be cured by personal
resolution. medical treatment or
moral suasion. People who wish
to drink liquor will do so in spite
of constitutions and statutes.


For eight years | resided in
Benton county. Arkansas, and
there was not a saloon In the
county nor could anyone buy a
drink of liquor at drug store. And
yet there was whisky everywhere.
II it was not shipped in from an
other state In cases. it was made
at home. And strange as 1t may
seem. the principal ''moonshiners'
of Benton county We're the pillars
of the local churches and all very
ardent advocates of prohibition.
Whisky is one of the simplest
beverages in the world to make. |
have seen a housewife In Arkan-
sas sitting In the kitchen knitting
